title: MODEL CERTIFICATE FOR FISH AND FISHERY PRODUCTS

sections.section: MODEL CERTIFICATE FOR FISH AND FISHERY PRODUCTS
sections.summary: Title/heading only: Model Certificate for Fish and Fishery Products (no substantive provisions in this section).
Document identifier only: CAC/GL 48-2004 (no substantive provisions in this section).

Explains that official certification can complement inspection systems to facilitate international trade, recommends limiting certificate types and using Codex model certificates, and encourages alternatives (e.g., equivalence assessments or mutual recognition/bilateral agreements) where exporting and importing inspection systems are assessed as equivalent.

States the model certificates apply to fish and fishery products for international trade that meet importing-country food safety, wholesomeness and production conformity requirements (excluding animal/plant health), allows electronic certificates if authorities agree on security, and indicates certificates should describe lots/batches and may attest to inspections, analyses, QA evaluations or product specifications.

Provides definitions for key terms used in the document including certification, certifying bodies and officers, inspection and inspection systems, official and officially recognized systems and certificates, and defines 'requirements' as the criteria set by competent authorities for trade in fish and fishery products.

sections.section: GENERAL  CONSIDERATIONS  CONCERNING  THE  PRODUCTION  AND  ISSUANCE  OF CERTIFICATES
sections.summary: Recommends that production and issuance of certificates follow specified Codex guidelines (listed) and that certificate language(s) be selected to meet the importing country's needs while ensuring certifying officer comprehension and minimizing burden on the exporter.

Advises that the Model Sanitary Certificate format (ANNEX I) should be used as a template when developing certificates to attest that fish and fishery products were produced in establishments under an exporting country's control or under conditions covered by equivalence/compliance agreements.

Requires every field of the Model Sanitary Certificate to be completed or otherwise marked to prevent alteration and specifies required elements and practices including a unique identification number (addenda must carry the same ID and certifying officer signature), country of dispatch, competent authority, certifying body, state/type of processing, packaging type, lot identifier/date code, transport details, an attestation of regulatory standing and HACCP/sanitary program, designation of original/copy/replacement, page numbering, and secure seals/signatures to minimize fraud.

sections.section: MODEL SANITARY CERTIFICATE COVERING FISH AND FISHERY PRODUCTS
sections.summary: Provides the template layout of the Model Sanitary Certificate including header fields (identification number, country of dispatch, competent authority, certifying body), section I product identification table (description, species, processing, packaging, lot/date, package counts, net weight, storage/transport temperature) and section II provenance (production establishment addresses/registration and consignor details).

sections.section: I. Details identifying the fishery products
sections.summary: Lists the product identification fields required in Section I of the certificate: product description, species (scientific name), state/type of processing, packaging type, lot identifier/date code, number of packages, net weight (and total) and the temperature required during storage and transport.

sections.section: II. Provenance of the fishery products
sections.summary: Requires Section II to list addresses and/or registration numbers of production establishments authorized for export by the competent authority and the name and address of the consignor.
Annex heading only: 'ANNEX I' serving as a reference to the model sanitary certificate annex (no substantive text here).

sections.section: III. Destination of the fishery products
sections.summary: Form requires recording the place of dispatch, country and place of destination, means of transport, and the consignee’s name and address for the fishery products shipment.

The certifying officer attests that the listed products originate from establishment(s) approved by or in good regulatory standing with the competent authority in the exporting country and that they have been handled, prepared/processed, identified, stored and transported under a competent HACCP and sanitary programme consistent with the Codex Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products, and provides place/date, seal, signature, officer name/position and contact details.
